title: TCE at Qur'an QA 2023 Shared Task: Low Resource Enhanced Transformer-based Ensemble Approach for Qur'anic QA
excerpt: In our recent work on the Qur'an QA 2023 shared tasks A and B, we tackled the challenge of limited training data by leveraging transfer learning and a voting ensemble to enhance prediction stability across multiple runs. We employed diverse architectures and learning mechanisms using various Arabic pre-trained transformer-based models for both tasks. To effectively identify unanswerable questions, we implemented a thresholding mechanism. Our top-performing systems significantly outperformed the baseline, achieving a MAP score of 25.05% for task A and a partial Average Precision (pAP) of 57.11% for task B, showcasing the robustness and effectiveness of our approach.

title: TCE at Qur'an QA 2022: Arabic Language Question Answering Over Holy Qur'an Using a Post-Processed Ensemble of BERT-based Models
excerpt:
In recent years, we've seen remarkable advancements in natural language understanding, driven by machine learning. One key area benefiting from these advancements is question answering, which enhances user experience on search engines and social media platforms. Given that Arabic, the language of the Holy Qur'an, is spoken by 1.8 billion people worldwide, it's crucial to develop effective NLP solutions for this complex language.
During the OSACT5 Qur'an QA 2022 Shared Task, we tackled this challenge by developing an ensemble learning model based on Arabic variants of BERT models. To boost accuracy, we also implemented advanced post-processing techniques. Our efforts resulted in a Partial Reciprocal Rank (pRR) score of 56.6% on the official test set, showcasing the potential of our approach in handling Arabic for question answering tasks.
